In years when Liberation Day isn't a national holiday, it is a holiday for the public sector and all schools will be closed. ## History of Liberation Day. At the outbreak of the second world war, the Netherlands had declared its neutrality from the conflict. Even though some German troops remained on Dutch soil until May 8th, the date of the surrender of the German forces is celebrated on Liberation Day.
